severity_rationale: Why this severity is justified after checking attacker role, preconditions, exploitability, impact scope, and deployment assumptions.
evidence_assessment: Whether the scenario evidence proves reachability, attacker control, sink or boundary, missing guard, and concrete impact.
dedupe_notes: How this compares to existing findings or related candidates.

finding.title: <severity> - <type of vuln> - <location>
finding.attacker_role: Required attacker role, auth state, or privilege level
finding.preconditions: Deployment settings, feature flags, data state, or user actions required
finding.summary: Technical summary of the verified root cause.
finding.evidence: Source, sink, missing guard, line references, and proof notes.
finding.attack_chain:
1. Attacker reaches the entrypoint.
2. Attacker controls the vulnerable input.
3. Input reaches the sink without the required guard.
4. Security impact occurs.
finding.example_attack: Concrete controlled-test example of how the attack would look, without unnecessary live-target weaponization.
finding.impact: Short impact statement retained for summary tooling.
finding.impact_analysis: Thorough impact analysis covering confidentiality, integrity, availability, privilege boundaries, data scope, and realistic blast radius.
finding.attacker_use: How an attacker could use this in practice, including what they gain and what limits the attack.
finding.validation_notes: How to reproduce safely and how to verify the fix.
